title: adenophora tetraphylla var. princeps
intro: Adenophora tetraphylla var. princeps, also known as Prince's Dragonhead or Prince's Aster, is a plant species in the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae) and is native to China. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. It is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating coughs and respiratory issues, and promoting the healing of wounds. Adenophora tetraphylla var. princeps can be prepared as an herbal tea or infused in hot water, and its roots and flowers are also used in traditional Chinese medicine preparations.
